channel.title: Your Name — Blog
channel.description: Build logs, competition notes, and project deep-dives.

item.title: I Qualified for the FBLA National Leadership Conference
item.description: I just qualified for FBLA NLC in Introduction to Programming, and here’s what the state-level presentation pressure felt like.

item.title: We Won First Place at GASTC with Cricket Coach
item.description: Shivam and I took first place at GASTC with Cricket Coach, and here’s how we built pose-to-coaching metrics and why the feedback mattered.

item.title: We Won the Cyberwave Hackathon
item.description: Shivam and I won the Cyberwave hackathon by building a web app to help people practice high-stress customer and interview scenarios.

item.title: MIT Zero Robotics Finals: 3rd Place Internationally (Alliance 10)
item.description: How we finished 3rd place internationally in the MIT Zero Robotics Finals as Alliance 10.

item.title: Building a 2D Fluid Sim in the Browser with WebGPU
item.description: Why I built a real-time Navier–Stokes fluid sim using WebGPU and WGSL, what I learned, and why I skipped Three.js.

item.title: Building VolSurf and Getting Into Quant
item.description: Why I built an implied volatility surface app, what I learned implementing Black–Scholes and the IV solver, and how much I'm enjoying quant so far.

item.title: Qualifying for GSEF with Field-Scale Frost & Drought Forecasting
item.description: How I qualified for the Georgia Science and Engineering Fair from my county fair with a high-resolution ML system for frost and drought prediction, and how I plan to refine it.

item.title: MIT Zero Robotics: Galactic Greenhouse — How We Became Finalists
item.description: A quick story about our strategy, what we learned, and what made Galactic Greenhouse fun (and hard).
